title: [cross-file] rx_ctx.peer_eui64 set before signature verify in peer_try_all_pubkeys
description:
Interaction: lichen_l2.c:peer_try_all_pubkeys <-> lichen_link_rx.c

Bug: In peer_try_all_pubkeys() (line 544-545):
  ctx->peer_pubkey = peer_table[i].pubkey;
  ctx->peer_eui64 = peer_table[i].eui64;

This sets ctx->peer_eui64 BEFORE calling lichen_link_rx() which verifies the signature.

Inside lichen_link_rx(), at lines 228-240, src_eui64 is derived from ctx->peer_pubkey (via SHA-256), NOT from ctx->peer_eui64. So ctx->peer_eui64 is unused for EUI-64 derivation.

BUT ctx->peer_eui64 IS used for MIC verification (lichen_link_rx.c line 98):
  build_link_nonce(nonce, ctx->peer_eui64, frame->epoch, frame->seqnum);

So the MIC verification uses peer_table[i].eui64 (set at line 545) while signature verification uses peer_table[i].pubkey (line 544).

The issue: peer_table stores eui64 that was provided by lichen_peer_add(). But lichen_link_rx derives src_eui64 from pubkey via SHA-256. If peer_table.eui64 doesn't match SHA-256(pubkey)[0:8], the MIC nonce will be wrong.

Actually, looking at lichen_peer_add (line 687-688):
  memcpy(peer_table[slot].eui64, eui64, LICHEN_EUI64_LEN);
  memcpy(peer_table[slot].pubkey, pubkey, LICHEN_L2_PUBKEY_LEN);

The caller provides both eui64 and pubkey. The code assumes they're correctly matched (eui64 = SHA-256(pubkey)[0:8]). If caller provides mismatched values, MIC verification will fail.

This isn't a bug per se - it's caller's responsibility. But there's no validation that eui64 == SHA-256(pubkey)[0:8].

Actually, this is a real issue: the EUI-64 in peer_table comes from the EDHOC handshake or announce processing. It's the address the remote node claims to have (their hwid-derived EUI-64). But lichen_link_rx derives EUI-64 from pubkey. These are different derivation methods!

Remote node's EUI-64: SHA-256(hwid)
lichen_link_rx derivation: SHA-256(pubkey)

Unless the remote node derives its EUI-64 from its pubkey (which lora_l2.c doesn't do - it uses hwid), the MIC nonce and the derived source EUI-64 will differ.

This is actually a significant cross-file inconsistency, not just documentation.
close_reason: Fixed: src_eui64 now uses ctx->peer_eui64 (from peer table) when available, ensuring consistency with MIC nonce. Fallback to pubkey-derived EUI-64 only when peer_eui64 is NULL, with warning about MIC incompatibility.
